ai_insights.executive_summary: CCI's network delivered mixed results in February 2026, with a portfolio average performance score of 55.6 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average), anchored by standout gains at Florence and 14th Ave but offset by persistent operational friction at the Pod locations. Florence's scheduler compliance improved 32.3 points from 55.8% in January to 88.1% in February, while 14th Ave's compliance rose 26.4 points from 66.5% in January to 92.9% in February—both now tracking above the company average of 78.27%. However, three Pod facilities remain below the 50-point threshold, with CCI-E Pod's average delay of 15.6 minutes running 147% above the company benchmark of 6.31 minutes.
highlights: Florence emerged as our strongest performer this month with a composite score of 85 out of 100, reflecting a 32.3-point gain in scheduler compliance from January (55.8%) to February (88.1%), alongside a concurrent 1.6-minute reduction in average delay from 3.8 minutes in January to 2.2 minutes in February. 14th Ave similarly demonstrated disciplined execution, advancing scheduler compliance 26.4 points from 66.5% in January to 92.9% in February—the highest compliance rate across the network—while reducing treatments running past closing from 0.7 daily in January to 0.5 daily in February, reflecting both tighter scheduling and improved end-of-day workflow. Both clinics achieved perfect or near-perfect iAssign utilization, suggesting that structured assignment protocols are accompanying the scheduling gains we see in the data.
concerns: We noticed that the Pod network—CCI-AB, CCI-E, CCI-CD, and CCI-Gh—exhibits a consistent pattern worth exploring together: each runs chair utilization between 76.3% and 90.4%, significantly above the company average of 71.91%, yet simultaneously carries average delays of 14.4 to 15.6 minutes, more than double the company benchmark of 6.31 minutes. This association suggests that high chair utilization without corresponding scheduling discipline is creating bottlenecks rather than efficiency; CCI-CD Pod specifically logged 3.3 treatments past closing daily, a 230% outlier against the clinic-by-clinic average of 1.0. Additionally, one Hospital Drive's chair utilization of 52.3% sits 19.6 points below the company average, creating potential capacity headroom that may warrant a review of volume distribution or scheduling patterns relative to its strong delay performance of 4.9 minutes.
recommendations: We recommend a focused operational audit at CCI-E Pod, where average delay of 15.6 minutes represents the largest performance gap in the network (147% above the company average of 6.31 minutes). Given that CCI-E's chair utilization is 90.4%—among the highest—yet iAssign data is unavailable, we suggest reviewing the clinic's scheduling template alignment with iOptimize recommendations and auditing whether nurse-to-chair ratios during peak hours are creating silent queues. A one-week pilot with tighter appointment spacing and iAssign enforcement could illuminate whether the delay stems from scheduling discipline or from capacity constraints masked by high utilization.

ai_insights.executive_summary: CCI's composite performance score of 44 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average) reflects a clinic operating under significant operational strain. While scheduler compliance improved notably—rising from 71.5% in February to 83.9% in March, a 17.3 percentage point gain—this improvement has not yet coincided with the patient flow metrics or overtime control seen at higher-performing sites like Florence (81/100) and 14th Ave (82/100). The core challenge remains CCI's 15.3-minute average delay paired with 3.4 daily treatments running past closing, both substantially elevated relative to the company average of 6.38 minutes and near-zero overtime at top performers.
highlights: CCI's scheduler compliance climb from 71.5% in February to 83.9% in March demonstrates strong execution momentum, narrowing the gap toward the company average of 88.14%. Within CCI's pod structure, the AB Pod showed particularly strong discipline, improving from 71.7% scheduler compliance in February to 85.2% in March—a decisive 18.9 percentage point move that suggests the scheduling interventions are taking root across the network.
concerns: We noticed that CCI's chair utilization sits at 83.3%, well above the company average of 76.1%, yet patient delays remain 2.4 times higher than the 6.38-minute company average. This pattern suggests the clinic may be operating near or at capacity without the scheduling precision needed to distribute that load smoothly; one area worth exploring together is whether appointment spacing templates are creating bunching later in the day that iOptimize recommendations would mitigate. Additionally, nurse utilization at 91.2%—the highest across all clinics—sits very close to maximum; this elevated staffing burn alongside persistent delays warrants a review of whether nurse-to-chair assignments are optimized or whether scheduling adjustments might reduce per-patient handling time.
recommendations: We recommend CCI focus immediate attention on the Gh Pod, which is experiencing a 15.6-minute average delay—144% above the company average of 6.38 minutes and the worst performance in the network. Specifically, we suggest auditing that pod's appointment scheduling templates within iOptimize to identify whether consecutive bookings are spaced to allow buffer time for setup and patient flow; even modest reductions in back-to-back stacking can compress delays without reducing volume. This targeted review aligns with CCI's recent 17.3-point compliance gain and positions the pod to convert that scheduling discipline into measurable relief in patient wait times.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
